normalized_block:
I'll examine the fixture to ground my review in the actual code.# Review: rs-11 — `RunContext::resolve`

**BLOCKER — `src/lib.rs:20-21`, `resolve` ignores `self.session_cwd` and joins against `std::env::current_dir()`.** The method reaches out to *global process state* (the bridge's launch cwd) instead of the struct's own pinned base, directly inverting the documented invariant ("resolve UNDER this base, never against the bridge process's own launch directory"). Concrete failure: one `serve` process launched from `/opt/bridge` handling a run pinned to `/repos/customer-a` calls `resolve("spec.md")` → returns `/opt/bridge/spec.md`, so the agent reads/writes a file in the wrong repo (or nonexistent path) for *every* run whose target repo isn't the launch dir — i.e. the normal case. The entire reason `RunContext` carries `session_cwd` is defeated. Fix: `self.session_cwd.join(rel)` and delete the `current_dir()` call.

**MAJOR (boundary safety, survives the BLOCKER fix) — `src/lib.rs:20-21`, no containment enforcement at the trust boundary.** `resolve` is the seam between untrusted task-spec input and the filesystem the agent touches, yet `PathBuf::join` performs no escape check. Even after fixing the base to `session_cwd`, `resolve("/etc/passwd")` returns `/etc/passwd` (an absolute `rel` replaces the base entirely) and `resolve("../../other-repo/secret")` walks out of the pinned repo. In a multi-repo `serve`, that is a cross-tenant read/write escape. A correctness-only pass checking "does it join under session_cwd" misses this because the code *looks* right after the one-line fix. Direction: canonicalize the joined path and assert `starts_with(session_cwd)` (or reject absolute/`..` components), so the invariant is *enforced*, not just intended.

**MAJOR (error-handling structure) — `src/lib.rs:19-22`, infallible signature launders failure into a silently-wrong path.** `resolve` returns a bare `PathBuf`, and `current_dir().unwrap_or_default()` converts an error into `PathBuf::default()` (empty), yielding a bogus relative path the agent then acts on with no signal. Once containment (above) is added, escape and resolution failures have no channel to surface. Direction: make it fallible (`Result<PathBuf, _>` / `Option`) so callers can distinguish "resolved and inside the repo" from "rejected/failed" instead of proceeding on garbage — turning a wrong filesystem op into a caught error.

**MINOR (symlink escape, only if the containment guarantee must be hard) — `src/lib.rs`.** A `starts_with` check on the un-canonicalized path still lets a symlink *inside* `session_cwd` redirect the agent outside it at the OS level. If `resolve` is meant to be a true sandbox boundary rather than a best-effort base, canonicalize before the prefix check; if it's only a convenience join, document that it is not a security boundary so no caller over-trusts it.

**Verdict:** Reject — the core `current_dir()`-vs-`session_cwd` bug breaks the stated contract on every non-launch-dir run (BLOCKER), and even fixed, `resolve` has no boundary enforcement or failure channel at a trust seam that untrusted task-spec paths cross.
